title: Worked examples
text: pgmm_rjmcmc() expects numeric matrix variables rows observations columns. code creates two compact clusters two dimensions.
code: library(bpgmm) #> bpgmm 1.1.6 loaded. If you use bpgmm in published work, please cite it with citation("bpgmm").  set.seed(2026)  X <- cbind(   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = -2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2),   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = 2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2) ) known_labels <- rep(1:2, each = 4)  dim(X) #> [1] 2 8 known_labels #> [1] 1 1 1 1 2 2 2 2

title: Worked examples
text: paper describes eight covariance structures using three-letter PGMM labels: CCC, CCU, CUC, CUU, UCC, UCU, UUC, UUU. Use model_to_constraint() calling sampler, constraint_to_model() reading stored results.
code: constraint <- model_to_constraint("UUU") constraint #> [1] 0 0 0  constraint_to_model(constraint) #> [1] "UUU"

title: Worked examples
text: vignette uses three posterior iterations runs quickly. research use, increase burn niter, set Mstep = 1 Vstep = 1 want RJMCMC model selection number clusters covariance structure. returned object stores posterior samples allocations, covariance constraints, component parameters, hyperparameters.
code: fit_log <- capture.output({   fit <- pgmm_rjmcmc(     X = X,     mInit = 2,     mVec = c(1, 3),     qnew = 1,     burn = 1,     niter = 3,     constraint = constraint,     Mstep = 0,     Vstep = 0,     verbose = FALSE   ) }) tail(fit_log, 1) #> character(0) names(fit) #>  [1] "taoList"        "psyList"        "MList"          #>  [4] "lambdaList"     "YList"          "ZmatList"       #>  [7] "constraintList" "alpha1Vec"      "alpha2Vec"      #> [10] "bbetaVec"       "clusIndList" length(fit$ZmatList) #> [1] 3

title: Worked examples
text: summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc() returns modal allocation, posterior counts number clusters, posterior counts covariance models, optionally adjusted Rand index.
code: fit_summary <- summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc(fit, trueCluster = known_labels)  fit_summary$Zalloc #> [1] 2 2 2 2 1 1 1 1 fit_summary$nCluster #>  #> 2  #> 3 fit_summary$nConstraint #>  #> UUU  #>   3 fit_summary$ari #> [1] 1

what: Scale the example to real data
title: Worked examples
text: real analysis, keep workflow use larger sampler settings. exact values depend data size convergence diagnostics.
code: fit <- pgmm_rjmcmc(   X = your_data_matrix,   mInit = 2,   mVec = c(1, 10),   qnew = 4,   burn = 5000,   niter = 15000,   constraint = model_to_constraint("UUU"),   Mstep = 1,   Vstep = 1,   SCind = 1,   verbose = FALSE )  summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc(fit, trueCluster = known_labels)

title: Getting started with bpgmm
text: Input data numeric matrix variables rows observations columns. small example two variables eight observations. intentionally short vignette runs quickly; use larger burn niter values real analysis. arguments control sampler: mInit initial number clusters. mVec allowed range number clusters. qnew number latent factors new cluster. constraint selects starting covariance model. Mstep = 1 allows RJMCMC updates number clusters. Vstep = 1 allows RJMCMC updates across covariance-constraint models. longer run applied work might look like :
code: library(bpgmm) #> bpgmm 1.1.6 loaded. If you use bpgmm in published work, please cite it with citation("bpgmm").  set.seed(2026)  X <- cbind(   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = -2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2),   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = 2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2) ) known_labels <- rep(1:2, each = 4)  fit_log <- capture.output({   fit <- pgmm_rjmcmc(     X = X,     mInit = 2,     mVec = c(1, 3),     qnew = 1,     burn = 1,     niter = 3,     constraint = model_to_constraint("UUU"),     Mstep = 0,     Vstep = 0,     verbose = FALSE   ) }) tail(fit_log, 1) #> character(0) fit <- pgmm_rjmcmc(   X = methylation_matrix,   mInit = 2,   mVec = c(1, 6),   qnew = 2,   burn = 100,   niter = 1000,   Mstep = 1,   Vstep = 1,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Getting started with bpgmm
text: reference partition available, pass trueCluster calculate adjusted Rand index.
code: summary <- summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc(fit, trueCluster = known_labels)  summary$Zalloc #> [1] 2 2 2 2 1 1 1 1 summary$nCluster #>  #> 2  #> 3 summary$nConstraint #>  #> UUU  #>   3 summary$ari #> [1] 1

title: Getting started with bpgmm
text: paper uses eight PGMM covariance model labels CCC, CCU, CUC, CUU, UCC, UCU, UUC, UUU. package stores internally three-number constraint vectors.
code: model_to_constraint("UUU") #> [1] 0 0 0 constraint_to_model(c(1, 0, 0)) #> [1] "CUU"

title: Getting started with bpgmm
text: old names pgmmRJMCMC(), summarizePgmmRJMCMC(), summerizePgmmRJMCMC() remain available compatibility wrappers, new code use pgmm_rjmcmc() summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc().

title: Getting started with bpgmm
text: use bpgmm published work, please cite package methodology paper: Lu, X., Li, Y., & Love, T. (2021). Bayesian Analysis Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models. Journal Classification, 38, 576-593. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00357-021-09391-8
code: citation("bpgmm") #> To cite package 'bpgmm' in publications use: #>  #>   Lu X, Li Y, Love T (2021). On Bayesian Analysis of #>   Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models. Journal of #>   Classification, 38, 576-593. doi:10.1007/s00357-021-09391-8 #>  #> A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is #>  #>   @Article{, #>     title = {On Bayesian Analysis of Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models}, #>     author = {Xiang Lu and Yaoxiang Li and Tanzy Love}, #>     journal = {Journal of Classification}, #>     year = {2021}, #>     volume = {38}, #>     pages = {576--593}, #>     doi = {10.1007/s00357-021-09391-8}, #>   }

title: Model and sampler details
text: Let X=(x1,…,xn)X = (x_1, \ldots, x_n) matrix continuous observations pp variables nn observations. bpgmm expects orientation: variables rows observations columns. cluster kk, mixture factor analyzers model writes xi=μk+Λkyki+ϵki, x_i = \mu_k + \Lambda_k y_{ki} + \epsilon_{ki}, yki∼N(0,Iqk),ϵki∼N(0,Ψk). y_{ki} \sim N(0, I_{q_k}), \qquad \epsilon_{ki} \sim N(0, \Psi_k). integrating latent factor ykiy_{ki}, cluster-specific covariance Σk=ΛkΛk⊤+Ψk. \Sigma_k = \Lambda_k \Lambda_k^\top + \Psi_k. mixture density f(xi)=∑k=1mτkN(xi∣μk,ΛkΛk⊤+Ψk), f(x_i) = \sum_{k = 1}^m \tau_k   N(x_i \mid \mu_k, \Lambda_k \Lambda_k^\top + \Psi_k), τk\tau_k mixture weight mm number clusters. pgmm_rjmcmc(): X p×np \times n data matrix. mInit starting value mm. mVec allowed range mm. qnew factor dimension assigned newly proposed cluster. constraint starting covariance model.

title: Model and sampler details
text: paper uses three letters describe covariance structure. letter either C constrained U unconstrained. eight PGMM models : package uses numeric encoding internally: 1 means constrained 0 means unconstrained.
code: library(bpgmm) #> bpgmm 1.1.6 loaded. If you use bpgmm in published work, please cite it with citation("bpgmm").  models <- c("CCC", "CCU", "CUC", "CUU", "UCC", "UCU", "UUC", "UUU") data.frame(   model = models,   constraint = vapply(     models,     function(x) paste(model_to_constraint(x), collapse = ","),     character(1)   ) ) #>     model constraint #> CCC   CCC      1,1,1 #> CCU   CCU      1,1,0 #> CUC   CUC      1,0,1 #> CUU   CUU      1,0,0 #> UCC   UCC      0,1,1 #> UCU   UCU      0,1,0 #> UUC   UUC      0,0,1 #> UUU   UUU      0,0,0 model_to_constraint("UUU") #> [1] 0 0 0 constraint_to_model(c(1, 1, 0)) #> [1] "CCU"

title: Model and sampler details
text: supplement gives natural conjugate priors used MCMC updates. compact form, main priors : τ∼Dirichlet(γ,…,γ), \tau \sim \mathrm{Dirichlet}(\gamma, \ldots, \gamma), yki∼N(0,Iqk), y_{ki} \sim N(0, I_{q_k}), μk∼N(x‾,α1−1Ψk), \mu_k \sim N(\bar{x}, \alpha_1^{-1} \Psi_k), λkj∼N(0,α2−1Ψk), \lambda_{kj} \sim N(0, \alpha_2^{-1} \Psi_k), ψkj∼IG(δ,β). \psi_{kj} \sim \mathrm{IG}(\delta, \beta). package samples hyperparameters α1\alpha_1, α2\alpha_2, β\beta, gamma hyperpriors controlled : dVec: shape parameters. sVec: rate parameters. delta: inverse-gamma shape noise covariance. ggamma: Dirichlet concentration mixture weights. allocation update uses posterior probability pki=τkN(xi∣μk,Ψk+ΛkΛk⊤)∑ℓ=1mτℓN(xi∣μℓ,Ψℓ+ΛℓΛℓ⊤). p_{ki} = \frac{   \tau_k N(x_i \mid \mu_k, \Psi_k + \Lambda_k\Lambda_k^\top) }{   \sum_{\ell = 1}^{m}   \tau_\ell N(x_i \mid \mu_\ell, \Psi_\ell + \Lambda_\ell\Lambda_\ell^\top) }. (zi1,…,zim)∣X,Θ,H∼Multinomial(1,p1i,…,pmi). (z_{i1}, \ldots, z_{im}) \mid X, \Theta, H   \sim \mathrm{Multinomial}(1, p_{1i}, \ldots, p_{mi}). implemented package’s allocation update also joint posterior includes product allocated mixture weights: p(Z∣τ)=∏=1nτzi. p(Z \mid \tau) = \prod_{= 1}^{n} \tau_{z_i}.

title: Model and sampler details
text: RJMCMC used number parameters changes number clusters covariance constraint changes. cluster-number moves : covariance-structure moves toggle one constraint time: toggle whether Λk\Lambda_k shared across clusters; toggle whether Ψk\Psi_k shared across clusters; toggle whether Ψk\Psi_k isotropic diagonal. pgmm_rjmcmc(): Mstep = 1 enables birth/death moves mm. SCind = 1 adds split/combine moves Mstep = 1. Vstep = 1 enables covariance-constraint moves.

title: Model and sampler details
text: following example deliberately small vignette can run quickly. real analyses, increase burn niter. examples keep Mstep = 0 Vstep = 0 make vignette fast. full model-selection run uses interface:
code: set.seed(2026)  X <- cbind(   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = -2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2),   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = 2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2) ) known_labels <- rep(1:2, each = 4)  fit <- pgmm_rjmcmc(   X = X,   mInit = 2,   mVec = c(1, 3),   qnew = 1,   burn = 1,   niter = 3,   constraint = model_to_constraint("UUU"),   Mstep = 0,   Vstep = 0,   verbose = FALSE )  fit_summary <- summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc(fit, trueCluster = known_labels) fit_summary$nCluster #>  #> 2  #> 3 fit_summary$nConstraint #>  #> UUU  #>   3 fit_summary$ari #> [1] 1 fit <- pgmm_rjmcmc(   X = your_data_matrix,   mInit = 5,   mVec = c(1, 10),   qnew = 4,   burn = 5000,   niter = 15000,   constraint = model_to_constraint("UUU"),   Mstep = 1,   Vstep = 1,   SCind = 1,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Model and sampler details
text: sampler returns posterior samples lists. commonly used fields : Use summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc() posterior summaries:
code: names(fit) #>  [1] "taoList"        "psyList"        "MList"          #>  [4] "lambdaList"     "YList"          "ZmatList"       #>  [7] "constraintList" "alpha1Vec"      "alpha2Vec"      #> [10] "bbetaVec"       "clusIndList" names(fit_summary) #> [1] "Zalloc"      "nCluster"    "nConstraint" "ari"

Journal of #>   Classification, 38, 576-593. doi:10.1007/s00357-021-09391-8 #>  #> A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is #>  #>   @Article{, #>     title = {On Bayesian Analysis of Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models}, #>     author = {Xiang Lu and Yaoxiang Li and Tanzy Love}, #>     journal = {Journal of Classification}, #>     year = {2021}, #>     volume = {38}, #>     pages = {576--593}, #>     doi = {10.1007/s00357-021-09391-8}, #>   }

what: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: bpgmm implements Bayesian inference parsimonious Gaussian mixture models. designed model-based clustering number clusters, object partition, cluster covariance structure inferential targets. package uses Markov chain Monte Carlo posterior estimation reversible-jump MCMC (RJMCMC) model selection across constrained mixtures factor analyzers.

title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: Install released version CRAN: Install development version GitHub: Load package:
code: install.packages("bpgmm") install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("YaoxiangLi/bpgmm") library(bpgmm)

title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: bpgmm useful want : estimate number clusters data; infer posterior cluster membership probabilities; compare parsimonious covariance structures; fit mixture--factor-analyzers models high-dimensional clustering; use RJMCMC move across models different parameter dimensions.

title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: example creates two small clusters, fits short RJMCMC chain, summarizes posterior samples. Real analyses use longer burn-posterior samples. X numeric matrix variables rows observations columns. Set Mstep = 1 allow RJMCMC updates number clusters Vstep = 1 allow updates variance structure. main user-facing function pgmm_rjmcmc(). Important settings include: mInit: initial number clusters. mVec: allowed cluster range, c(1, 6). qnew: number latent factors newly proposed cluster. burn niter: burn-posterior sampling iterations. constraint: initial covariance model, usually set model_to_constraint(). Mstep, Vstep, SCind: switches cluster-number, covariance-model, split/combine RJMCMC moves. verbose: set FALSE suppress per-iteration progress output. older names pgmmRJMCMC(), summarizePgmmRJMCMC(), misspelled summerizePgmmRJMCMC() deprecated compatibility wrappers. still work, new code use pgmm_rjmcmc() summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc(). eight covariance structures paper represented model labels "CCC", "CUU", "UUU". package also accepts legacy three-number constraint encoding:
code: set.seed(2026)  X <- cbind(   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = -2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2),   matrix(rnorm(8, mean = 2, sd = 0.2), nrow = 2) ) known_labels <- rep(1:2, each = 4)  fit <- pgmm_rjmcmc(   X = X,   mInit = 2,   mVec = c(1, 3),   qnew = 1,   burn = 1,   niter = 3,   constraint = model_to_constraint("UUU"),   Mstep = 0,   Vstep = 0,   verbose = FALSE )  fit_summary <- summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc(fit, trueCluster = known_labels) as.integer(fit_summary$nCluster["2"]) #> [1] 3  as.integer(fit_summary$nConstraint["UUU"]) #> [1] 3  fit_summary$ari #> [1] 1 model_to_constraint("UUU") constraint_to_model(c(1, 0, 0))

title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: methodology behind package described : Lu, X., Li, Y., & Love, T. (2021). Bayesian Analysis Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models. Journal Classification, 38, 576-593. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00357-021-09391-8 paper develops RJMCMC inferential procedure constrained mixture--factor-analyzers models. inferential goals partition observations, number clusters, covariance structure clusters, represented posterior distributions.

title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: use bpgmm published work, please cite package methodology paper. R, run: BibTeX paper:
code: citation("bpgmm") @article{lu2021bayesian,   author = {Lu, Xiang and Li, Yaoxiang and Love, Tanzy},   title = {On Bayesian Analysis of Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models},   journal = {Journal of Classification},   year = {2021},   volume = {38},   pages = {576--593},   doi = {10.1007/s00357-021-09391-8} }

title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: bpgmm released GPL-3 license.

title: bpgmm: Bayesian inference for parsimonious Gaussian mixture models
text: package website available https://yaoxiangli.github.io/bpgmm/. includes reference index, release notes, worked examples run actual package functions.

what: Hyperparameter set for the Bayesian PGMM sampler. — Hparam-class
title: Hyperparameter set for the Bayesian PGMM sampler. — Hparam-class
text: Hyperparameter set Bayesian PGMM sampler.

title: Hyperparameter set for the Bayesian PGMM sampler. — Hparam-class
text: alpha1 First Dirichlet prior parameter component weights. alpha2 Second Dirichlet prior parameter component weights. delta Shape parameter used prior updates. ggamma Prior rate parameter used covariance updates. bbeta Prior scale parameter used covariance updates.

title: ThetaYList-class — ThetaYList
text: Parameter set sampled PGMM component parameters.

title: ThetaYList-class — ThetaYList
text: tao Numeric vector component mixing weights. psy List diagonal noise covariance matrices. M List component mean vectors. lambda List component factor loading matrices. Y List latent factor score matrices.

what: Convert PGMM Constraint Codes to Paper Model Names — constraint_to_model
title: Convert PGMM Constraint Codes to Paper Model Names — constraint_to_model
text: paper represents eight PGMM covariance structures three-letter model names. letter either `C` constrained `U` unconstrained. letters indicate whether loading matrix shared across clusters, whether noise covariance shared across clusters, whether noise covariance isotropic within clusters.

title: Convert PGMM Constraint Codes to Paper Model Names — constraint_to_model
code: constraint_to_model(constraint)

title: Convert PGMM Constraint Codes to Paper Model Names — constraint_to_model
text: constraint Integer numeric vector length three entries `0` `1`. `1` maps `C`; `0` maps `U`.

title: Convert PGMM Constraint Codes to Paper Model Names — constraint_to_model
text: character scalar, one `CCC`, `CCU`, `CUC`, `CUU`, `UCC`,   `UCU`, `UUC`, `UUU`.

what: Convert PGMM Paper Model Names to Constraint Codes — model_to_constraint
title: Convert PGMM Paper Model Names to Constraint Codes — model_to_constraint
text: Convert PGMM Paper Model Names Constraint Codes

title: Convert PGMM Paper Model Names to Constraint Codes — model_to_constraint
code: model_to_constraint(model)

title: Convert PGMM Paper Model Names to Constraint Codes — model_to_constraint
text: model Character scalar naming one eight PGMM covariance structures: `CCC`, `CCU`, `CUC`, `CUU`, `UCC`, `UCU`, `UUC`, `UUU`.

title: Convert PGMM Paper Model Names to Constraint Codes — model_to_constraint
text: Integer vector length three. `1` means constrained `0` means   unconstrained.

what: Bayesian Model-Based Clustering with Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models — pgmm_rjmcmc
title: Bayesian Model-Based Clustering with Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models — pgmm_rjmcmc
text: Carries model-based clustering using parsimonious Gaussian mixture models. MCMC used parameter estimation RJMCMC used model selection.

title: Bayesian Model-Based Clustering with Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models — pgmm_rjmcmc
code: pgmm_rjmcmc(   X,   mInit,   mVec,   qnew,   delta = 2,   ggamma = 2,   burn = 20,   niter = 1000,   constraint = c(0, 0, 0),   dVec = c(1, 1, 1),   sVec = c(1, 1, 1),   Mstep = 0,   Vstep = 0,   SCind = 0,   verbose = TRUE )  pgmmRJMCMC(   X,   mInit,   mVec,   qnew,   delta = 2,   ggamma = 2,   burn = 20,   niter = 1000,   constraint = c(0, 0, 0),   dVec = c(1, 1, 1),   sVec = c(1, 1, 1),   Mstep = 0,   Vstep = 0,   SCind = 0,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Bayesian Model-Based Clustering with Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models — pgmm_rjmcmc
text: X observation matrix size p * m mInit number initial clusters mVec range number clusters qnew number latent factors new cluster delta scalar hyperparameter noise covariance prior ggamma scalar hyperparameter used covariance-structure proposals burn number burn-iterations niter number posterior sampling iterations constraint initial PGMM covariance constraint. Use three-letter model label `"CCC"` `"UUU"`, numeric vector length three binary entries. example, `c(1, 1, 1)` `CCC`, fully constrained model, `c(0, 0, 0)` `UUU`, fully unconstrained model. dVec vector hyperparameters length three, shape parameters alpha1, alpha2 bbeta respectively sVec vector hyperparameters length three, rate parameters alpha1, alpha2 bbeta respectively Mstep indicator RJMCMC model selection number clusters Vstep indicator RJMCMC model selection covariance structures SCind indicator using split/combine moves cluster-number RJMCMC step verbose logical; `TRUE`, print iteration progress.

title: Bayesian Model-Based Clustering with Parsimonious Gaussian Mixture Models — pgmm_rjmcmc
text: `constraint` argument follows three-letter PGMM model notation used Lu, Li, Love (2021). first entry indicates whether loading matrices shared across clusters, second whether noise covariance matrices shared across clusters, third whether noise covariance isotropic within cluster. Use [model_to_constraint()] convert model names `CCC`, `CCU`, `CUC`, `CUU`, `UCC`, `UCU`, `UUC`, `UUU` numeric vector used internally.

what: Summarize RJMCMC Samples from a Bayesian PGMM Fit — summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc
title: Summarize RJMCMC Samples from a Bayesian PGMM Fit — summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc
text: Summarizes posterior samples [pgmm_rjmcmc()] modal allocation, posterior counts number clusters, posterior counts eight PGMM covariance-constraint models, optionally adjusted Rand index known reference partition.

title: Summarize RJMCMC Samples from a Bayesian PGMM Fit — summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc
code: summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc(pgmmResList, trueCluster = NULL)  summarizePgmmRJMCMC(pgmmResList, trueCluster = NULL)  summerizePgmmRJMCMC(pgmmResList, trueCluster = NULL)

title: Summarize RJMCMC Samples from a Bayesian PGMM Fit — summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc
text: pgmmResList Result list [pgmm_rjmcmc()]. trueCluster Optional true reference cluster allocation.

title: Summarize RJMCMC Samples from a Bayesian PGMM Fit — summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc
text: list `Zalloc`, `nCluster`, `nConstraint`, optionally   `ari`.

title: bpgmm 1.1.6
text: Added model--sampler vignette explains mixture--factor-analyzers likelihood, PGMM covariance constraints, conjugate priors, allocation update, RJMCMC moves, package output fields formulas runnable examples. Linked new mathematical vignette getting-started guide pkgdown article index.

title: bpgmm 1.1.5
text: Fixed allocation prior contribution RJMCMC acceptance calculations use log product allocated mixture weights, matching paper’s joint posterior.

title: bpgmm 1.1.4
text: Standardized internal R helper names snake_case preserving exported compatibility wrappers. Added snake_case wrappers around generated Rcpp entry points routed package internals wrappers. Cleaned source layout renaming R files lowercase hyphenated names removing ignored duplicate R/choosem/ tree. Kept result-list names legacy public arguments stable avoid breaking existing user code.

title: bpgmm 1.1.3
text: Fixed Mstep = 1 loading cluster-number proposal helpers package code rather leaving ignored nested R/ directory. Fixed split/combine RJMCMC moves passing X explicitly split-allocation helpers adding missing latent-factor update helper. Fixed C++ allocation update add log(tao) component log densities instead adding raw mixture weights. Added C++ input validation multivariate normal densities, log-ratio calculations, allocation dimensions, mixture weights. Expanded unit coverage eight PGMM covariance constraints, C++ helpers, cluster-number RJMCMC, covariance RJMCMC, split/combine moves.

title: bpgmm 1.1.2
text: Modernized README badges, installation guidance, paper citation, model-constraint helper examples. Added summarizePgmmRJMCMC() correctly spelled summary function retained summerizePgmmRJMCMC() backward compatibility. Added tidyverse-style pgmm_rjmcmc() summarize_pgmm_rjmcmc() preferred public API. Deprecated pgmmRJMCMC(), summarizePgmmRJMCMC(), summerizePgmmRJMCMC(); names remain available compatibility wrappers. Added helpers model_to_constraint() constraint_to_model() translating paper model labels legacy constraint vectors. Improved package startup citation guidance users publishing results bpgmm. Added unit tests public API, covariance-constraint mapping, summary helpers, native C++ wrappers. Fixed zero-iteration handling pgmm_rjmcmc() added validation sampler inputs summary result objects. Added verbose argument suppress per-iteration progress output examples, tests, scripted workflows. Updated C++ build settings validation current R/Rcpp best practices.
